PMEL._method: log1p(nCPM*6000/1e6)->Glassman(0.7768,3.9723); anchor=max nCPM over HPA celltypes per lineage. PMEL is a melanocyte/melanoma differentiation antigen: the malignant/tumor lineage (melanocyte-derived tumor cells; skin ABM ref_celltype='malignant cell') is keyed to HPA single-cell Melanocytes nCPM=3051.8 -> 1871303.4 copies/cell (81% of Glassman IHC-3 ceiling). All immune/stromal/epithelial lineages -> _default (HPA: 'Not detected in immune cells'; RNA blood lineage 'Not detected'; PMEL absent on keratinocytes/'epithelial cell'). Aliases 'malignant','malignant cell','Melanocytes' all carry the SAME sourced value so the melanoma build matches whichever key it coarsens the malignant melanocyte lineage to; keratinocytes and 'Other' deliberately left at _default to prevent off-target leakage (same fail-closed rule as the IHC path).

_sources.aliases: SILV, gp100, PMEL17, ME20, Pmel17, SI, SIL, HMB45
_sources.local_snapshot: hpa_full/proteinatlas.tsv (HPA bulk download; values verified identical to live HPA API 2026-07-13)
_sources.Glassman_ladder_ref: Glassman & Balthasar 2016, J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn 43:427-446, DOI 10.1007/s10928-016-9482-0 (IHC0..3 -> 9.36e3/6.46e4/2.55e5/2.30e6 copies/cell); _default=9.36e3 = IHC-0 floor
_sources.malignant_lineage_label: skin_abm_cells.npz ref_celltype='malignant cell' (n=19580, all is_tum=True); melanocyte->malignant per melanoma build

sources.retrieved_via: get_protein_atlas_gene (protein-annotation MCP, HPA release 25.1); values are verbatim HPA JSON fields. Independently re-verified by orchestrator (same MCP call).
identity_notes: RNA tissue: Tissue enhanced (skin 109.1 nTPM, cervix 31.6). Cell-type enrichment: Skin-Melanocytes.
Protein tissue: Not detected in normal tissue. Melanoma/melanocyte differentiation antigen (gp100), tebentafusp target.

values_used.anchor_choice: Melanocytes nCPM=3051.8 -> malignant/melanocyte lineage; all others floored to _default (IHC0=9360)
